project.name: netty-buffer
project.description: The netty-buffer module of Netty 4.2.x: provides the ByteBuf abstraction and buffer-pool primitives (allocators, pooled/unpooled heap and direct buffers) that underpin Netty's zero-copy I/O.

nodes.name: pom.xml
nodes.summary: Maven build configuration for the netty-buffer module declaring dependencies on netty-common and JCTools and inheriting build settings from the parent Netty POM.

nodes.name: AbstractAllocatorEvent.java
nodes.summary: Base class for allocator-level events that carries a reference to the originating ByteBufAllocator.

nodes.name: AbstractBufferEvent.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base for events related to individual buffers, exposing the buffer size as a common attribute.

nodes.name: AbstractByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Skeletal ByteBuf implementation centralizing reader/writer index management, bounds checking, primitive read/write dispatch, and most ByteBuf API contract behavior.
nodes.languageNotes: Roughly 200 methods drive nearly every concrete ByteBuf via getByte/setByte abstract hooks; bounds-check helpers (checkIndex, checkReadableBytes) are hot-path.

nodes.name: AbstractByteBufAllocator.java
nodes.summary: Skeletal ByteBufAllocator implementation that selects heap vs direct allocation, applies leak detection wrappers, and grows buffers via calculateNewCapacity.

nodes.name: AbstractChunkEvent.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base for memory-chunk events, carrying the chunk size for pool-instrumentation listeners.

nodes.name: AbstractDerivedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Common base for derived buffers (slices, duplicates) that delegate storage to a parent ByteBuf and share its reference count.

nodes.name: AbstractPooledDerivedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Base for pooled derived buffers (pooled slice/duplicate) that maintain an independent reference count so the underlying pooled chunk is released only when all derivations are freed.

nodes.name: AbstractReferenceCountedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: ByteBuf base implementing atomic, lock-free reference counting via ReferenceCountUpdater for safe shared ownership.

nodes.name: AbstractUnpooledSlicedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Base for unpooled slice buffers that re-map indices into a sub-range of a parent buffer while sharing its storage and reference count.

nodes.name: AbstractUnsafeSwappedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Byte-order-swapped view over an unsafe-backed ByteBuf that performs endian conversion using sun.misc.Unsafe intrinsics for speed.

nodes.name: AdaptiveByteBufAllocator.java
nodes.summary: ByteBufAllocator that delegates to an AdaptivePoolingAllocator, dynamically choosing pooling strategy based on observed allocation patterns.

nodes.name: AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java
nodes.summary: Self-tuning buffer pool that combines per-thread magazine caches with global chunk recycling and size-class adaptation to balance throughput, fragmentation, and memory footprint.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of JCTools queues, VarHandles, and thread-local Magazine objects; the 2k-line class implements its own size-class histogram to retune chunk sizes online.

nodes.name: AdvancedLeakAwareByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Leak-detecting ByteBuf wrapper that records every API call into the associated ResourceLeakTracker so leak reports include the full access trace.

nodes.name: AdvancedLeakAwareCompositeByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Leak-tracing wrapper for CompositeByteBuf that records every composite-buffer operation into the leak tracker for diagnostic reporting.

nodes.name: AllocateBufferEvent.java
nodes.summary: Event fired whenever a buffer is allocated, carrying the allocator and the size requested.

nodes.name: AllocateChunkEvent.java
nodes.summary: Event fired when a new memory chunk is allocated inside the pool, used for pool-instrumentation listeners.

nodes.name: ByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Public abstract ByteBuf API: Netty's central byte-container abstraction defining reader/writer indices, primitive accessors, slicing, retention, and the contract every concrete buffer must satisfy.
nodes.languageNotes: Around 2.5k lines of API surface; almost every other class in this module either implements or wraps ByteBuf.

nodes.name: ByteBufAllocator.java
nodes.summary: Allocator interface that produces heap, direct, and composite ByteBufs, abstracting over pooled and unpooled implementations.

nodes.name: ByteBufAllocatorMetric.java
nodes.summary: Read-only metrics view exposing total heap- and direct-memory currently used by an allocator.

nodes.name: ByteBufAllocatorMetricProvider.java
nodes.summary: Marker interface for allocators that expose a ByteBufAllocatorMetric for monitoring.

nodes.name: ByteBufConvertible.java
nodes.summary: Interface for objects that can expose themselves as a ByteBuf without performing a copy.

nodes.name: ByteBufHolder.java
nodes.summary: Interface for objects that contain a ByteBuf payload, defining content access, copy, and reference-counted lifecycle methods.

nodes.name: ByteBufInputStream.java
nodes.summary: InputStream/DataInput adapter that reads from a ByteBuf, advancing the reader index and optionally releasing the buffer on close.

nodes.name: ByteBufOutputStream.java
nodes.summary: OutputStream/DataOutput adapter that writes into a ByteBuf, growing it as needed and tracking written bytes.

nodes.name: AbstractByteBuf
nodes.summary: Skeletal ByteBuf implementing index management, bounds checks, and most read/write/search/transfer logic on top of subclass-provided primitive accessors.

nodes.name: AbstractByteBufAllocator
nodes.summary: Skeletal allocator routing heap vs direct allocation, wrapping buffers with leak-detection variants, and providing growth policy via calculateNewCapacity.

nodes.name: AbstractDerivedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Base for derived buffers that share the parent's storage and reference count without owning memory.

nodes.name: AbstractPooledDerivedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Pooled derived buffer base whose independent refcount keeps the underlying pooled chunk alive until every slice/duplicate is released.

nodes.name: AbstractReferenceCountedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Atomic reference-counted ByteBuf base that delegates to ReferenceCountUpdater for lock-free retain/release.

nodes.name: AbstractUnpooledSlicedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Unpooled slice base mapping indices into a parent buffer sub-range while sharing storage and refcount.

nodes.name: AbstractUnsafeSwappedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Byte-swap view over an unsafe-backed buffer using sun.misc.Unsafe intrinsics for fast endian conversion.

nodes.name: AdaptiveByteBufAllocator
nodes.summary: ByteBufAllocator front-end that delegates allocations to an AdaptivePoolingAllocator instance.

nodes.name: AdaptivePoolingAllocator
nodes.summary: Self-tuning pool with per-thread magazines, global chunk recycling, and adaptive size classes for predictable throughput under varied workloads.

nodes.name: AdvancedLeakAwareByteBuf
nodes.summary: Wrapper that records each ByteBuf call site into the leak tracker so PARANOID-level leak reports include full access traces.

nodes.name: AdvancedLeakAwareCompositeByteBuf
nodes.summary: Leak-tracing wrapper for CompositeByteBuf that records every composite-buffer operation into the leak tracker.

nodes.name: ByteBuf
nodes.summary: Core ByteBuf abstract class defining the full byte-container contract: indices, primitive accessors, slicing, retention, and capacity policy.

nodes.name: ByteBufAllocator
nodes.summary: Interface for allocating heap, direct, and composite ByteBufs.

nodes.name: ByteBufInputStream
nodes.summary: InputStream/DataInput adapter over a ByteBuf with optional close-time release semantics.

nodes.name: ByteBufOutputStream
nodes.summary: OutputStream/DataOutput adapter that writes into and grows an underlying ByteBuf.

nodes.name: AbstractAllocatorEvent
nodes.summary: Base event holding the originating ByteBufAllocator.

nodes.name: AbstractBufferEvent
nodes.summary: Base buffer event carrying buffer size.

nodes.name: AbstractChunkEvent
nodes.summary: Base chunk event carrying chunk size.

nodes.name: AllocateBufferEvent
nodes.summary: Event fired when a buffer is allocated.

nodes.name: AllocateChunkEvent
nodes.summary: Event fired when a new memory chunk is allocated.

nodes.name: ByteBufAllocatorMetric
nodes.summary: Read-only metric view exposing used heap and direct memory.

nodes.name: ByteBufAllocatorMetricProvider
nodes.summary: Marker interface for allocators exposing a metric instance.

nodes.name: ByteBufConvertible
nodes.summary: Interface for zero-copy ByteBuf conversion.

nodes.name: ByteBufHolder
nodes.summary: Holder interface for objects wrapping a ByteBuf payload with refcount lifecycle.

nodes.name: ByteBufProcessor.java
nodes.summary: Deprecated marker interface extending ByteProcessor that defines reusable byte predicates for ByteBuf.forEachByte traversal.

nodes.name: ByteBufProcessor
nodes.summary: Interface defining the ByteBufProcessor contract used by the buffer module.

nodes.name: ByteBufUtil.java
nodes.summary: Static utility hub for ByteBuf operations: hex dump, equals/compare, encoding/decoding, hashing, search, swap and writeUtf8/Ascii helpers used across the buffer module.

nodes.name: ByteBufUtil
nodes.summary: ByteBufUtil class implementation: static utility hub for bytebuf operations: hex dump, equals/compare, encoding/decoding, hashing, search, swap and writeutf8/ascii helpers used across the buffer module.

nodes.name: PooledSlicedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Pooled implementation of a sliced view over another ByteBuf, returning slices to the recycler pool when released to avoid allocation overhead.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Netty's ObjectPool/Recycler pattern to reuse slice wrappers across requests.

nodes.name: PooledSlicedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Recyclable sliced ByteBuf that delegates reads/writes to a parent buffer with an adjusted index offset, returned to the object pool on deallocate.

nodes.name: PooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Pooled direct ByteBuf that uses sun.misc.Unsafe for fast off-heap memory access via raw memory addresses.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Unsafe intrinsics for byte/short/int/long get/set bypassing JNI ByteBuffer overhead.

nodes.name: PooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf
nodes.summary: Direct-memory pooled ByteBuf accessed through Unsafe memory address operations for low-latency I/O.

nodes.name: PooledUnsafeHeapByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Pooled heap-backed ByteBuf that uses Unsafe to access the underlying byte array at native speeds.

nodes.name: PooledUnsafeHeapByteBuf
nodes.summary: Heap-array pooled ByteBuf accelerated by Unsafe array element access for primitive get/set operations.

nodes.name: PoolSubpage.java
nodes.summary: Manages allocation of small fixed-size element runs within a single page by tracking free slots via a bitmap.
nodes.languageNotes: Bitmap-based free-slot tracking enables O(1) sub-page allocation/free for the small-size class.

nodes.name: PoolSubpage
nodes.summary: Doubly-linked subpage entry holding a bitmap of free element slots that satisfies small allocations within a chunk page.

nodes.name: PoolSubpageMetric.java
nodes.summary: Read-only metric interface exposing element size, max elements, and free element counts for a single PoolSubpage.

nodes.name: PoolSubpageMetric
nodes.summary: Interface providing observability into a subpage's size, capacity, and free-element count for monitoring tools.

nodes.name: PoolThreadCache.java
nodes.summary: Per-thread cache of recently freed pool buffer regions that avoids contended arena access on subsequent allocations.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements MPSC-style thread-local caching with periodic trim to balance memory retention versus hit rate.

nodes.name: PoolThreadCache
nodes.summary: Holds per-thread MemoryRegionCache arrays for small and normal size classes to serve allocations without arena locking.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyAbstractByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Abstract ByteBuf base that throws ReadOnlyBufferException on every mutating operation while leaving reads to subclasses.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyAbstractByteBuf
nodes.summary: Common parent for read-only ByteBuf variants that overrides every setter to raise ReadOnlyBufferException.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Immutable read-only wrapper around another ByteBuf that delegates reads and rejects all writes.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyByteBuf
nodes.summary: Wrapping ByteBuf that exposes a delegate buffer through read-only semantics by forwarding accessors and blocking mutators.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyByteBufferBuf.java
nodes.summary: Read-only ByteBuf backed by a java.nio.ByteBuffer, supporting both direct and heap NIO buffers as data sources.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges Netty's ByteBuf API onto the JDK's NIO ByteBuffer for interop with file/channel APIs.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyByteBufferBuf
nodes.summary: Adapts a java.nio.ByteBuffer as a read-only Netty ByteBuf, delegating index access and channel I/O to the underlying NIO buffer.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyUnsafeDirectByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Read-only direct ByteBuf variant that uses Unsafe memory address access for high-throughput zero-copy reads.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyUnsafeDirectByteBuf
nodes.summary: Read-only direct buffer that reads primitives from the underlying NIO buffer's native address using Unsafe.

nodes.name: ReallocateBufferEvent.java
nodes.summary: JFR event capturing buffer reallocation events with old and new capacities for performance profiling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Java Flight Recorder @Name/@Label annotations to register a custom profiling event.

nodes.name: ReallocateBufferEvent
nodes.summary: JFR Event subclass that records buffer reallocation old/new size for runtime performance analysis.

nodes.name: AbstractMultiSearchProcessorFactory.java

nodes.name: AbstractMultiSearchProcessorFactory

nodes.name: AbstractSearchProcessorFactory.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base factory exposing static builders for single-needle ByteBuf search using KMP or Bitap algorithms.

nodes.name: AbstractSearchProcessorFactory
nodes.summary: Static factory entry point that builds KMP or Bitap single-pattern search processor factories for ByteBufs.

nodes.name: AhoCorasicSearchProcessorFactory.java
nodes.summary: Builds Aho-Corasic automatons that simultaneously search a ByteBuf for many byte-string needles in linear time.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the Aho-Corasic trie with failure links, optimized for primitive byte alphabets.

nodes.name: AhoCorasicSearchProcessorFactory
nodes.summary: Compiles a list of byte-string needles into an Aho-Corasic automaton and supplies matching processors for ByteBuf scans.

nodes.name: BitapSearchProcessorFactory.java
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs Bitap (shift-or) search processors for sub-64-byte needles using bitmask state encoding.

nodes.name: BitapSearchProcessorFactory
nodes.summary: Precomputes the Bitap bitmask table for a needle and builds per-scan SearchProcessor instances.

nodes.name: KmpSearchProcessorFactory.java
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs Knuth-Morris-Pratt search processors using a precomputed jump table for efficient byte-string matching.

nodes.name: KmpSearchProcessorFactory
nodes.summary: Builds KMP search processors over a byte needle by precomputing the longest-prefix-suffix failure table.

nodes.name: MultiSearchProcessor.java
nodes.summary: Marker interface for ByteProcessors that can report which of several needles matched at the current position.

nodes.name: MultiSearchProcessorFactory.java
nodes.summary: Marker interface for factories that build multi-needle search processors over ByteBufs.

nodes.name: package-info.java
nodes.summary: Package documentation for io.netty.buffer.search describing the available KMP, Bitap, and Aho-Corasic search algorithms.

nodes.name: SearchProcessor.java
nodes.summary: Marker interface for stateful ByteProcessors that match a fixed needle while scanning a ByteBuf byte-by-byte.

nodes.name: SearchProcessorFactory.java
nodes.summary: Marker interface for factories producing SearchProcessor instances bound to a precomputed needle.

nodes.name: SimpleLeakAwareByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Wraps a ByteBuf with a ResourceLeakTracker so that unreleased buffers are reported by Netty's leak detector.

nodes.name: SimpleLeakAwareByteBuf
nodes.summary: Forwarding ByteBuf that closes its ResourceLeakTracker on release and re-wraps derived buffers to keep tracking intact.

nodes.name: SimpleLeakAwareCompositeByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Leak-aware wrapper for CompositeByteBuf that registers a ResourceLeakTracker and closes it on full release.

nodes.name: SimpleLeakAwareCompositeByteBuf

nodes.name: SizeClasses.java
nodes.summary: Precomputes the jemalloc-style size-class table used by the pool allocator to map requested sizes to fixed buckets.
nodes.languageNotes: Derives a log/linear size-class table from pageSize, pageShifts, chunkSize, and groupSize parameters.

nodes.name: SizeClasses
nodes.summary: Computes and exposes size-class lookups (size to index, index to size, normalization) used by the pool to route allocations.

nodes.name: SizeClassesMetric.java
nodes.summary: Read-only metric interface exposing size-class table conversions (size-to-index, normalization helpers) for diagnostics.

nodes.name: SizeClassesMetric
nodes.summary: Public-facing interface for SizeClasses that exposes size lookup methods without revealing internal computation.

nodes.name: SlicedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Deprecated thin subclass of AbstractUnpooledSlicedByteBuf kept for backwards compatibility; new code should use the abstract base directly.

nodes.name: SlicedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Deprecated public ByteBuf slice wrapper retained to avoid breaking older Netty API consumers.

nodes.name: SwappedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Deprecated wrapper that flips a ByteBuf's byte order by delegating every read/write to the underlying buffer with byte-swapping applied.
nodes.languageNotes: Massive override surface (~188 methods) mirrors every ByteBuf accessor with order-swap semantics.

nodes.name: SwappedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Delegating ByteBuf that exposes opposite endianness by intercepting every numeric accessor and applying byte swaps before delegating.

nodes.name: Unpooled.java
nodes.summary: Static factory facade for creating unpooled heap, direct, composite, wrapped, and copied ByteBuf instances; the canonical entry point for non-pooled buffer creation.

nodes.name: Unpooled
nodes.summary: Public static factory exposing buffer(), directBuffer(), wrappedBuffer(), copiedBuffer(), and related helpers built on top of UnpooledByteBufAllocator.DEFAULT.

nodes.name: UnpooledByteBufAllocator.java
nodes.summary: ByteBufAllocator implementation that allocates fresh heap and direct buffers per call without pooling, with optional unsafe and no-cleaner variants.

nodes.name: UnpooledByteBufAllocator
nodes.summary: Extends AbstractByteBufAllocator to provide non-pooled heap/direct allocation, selecting unsafe or safe implementations based on platform support.

nodes.name: UnpooledDirectByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Safe ByteBuf backed by a JDK direct ByteBuffer, providing full ByteBuf accessors without using sun.misc.Unsafe.

nodes.name: UnpooledDirectByteBuf
nodes.summary: Direct ByteBuf implementation that wraps a java.nio.ByteBuffer, manages its lifecycle, and exposes the full ByteBuf API via NIO calls.

nodes.name: UnpooledDuplicatedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Duplicate view of an AbstractByteBuf with independent reader/writer indices, used by Unpooled buffers to avoid pooled-buffer overhead.

nodes.name: UnpooledDuplicatedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends AbstractDuplicatedByteBuf with overrides tailored for non-pooled parent buffers and exposes typed get/set primitives.

nodes.name: UnpooledHeapByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Heap-array backed ByteBuf without pooling; uses a plain byte[] for storage and HeapByteBufUtil for typed accessors.

nodes.name: UnpooledHeapByteBuf
nodes.summary: Concrete unpooled heap ByteBuf managing a byte[] array, supporting reallocation via the parent allocator and standard reference counting.

nodes.name: UnpooledSlicedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Slice view over an AbstractByteBuf optimised for non-pooled parents, exposing a sub-range with shared underlying storage.

nodes.name: UnpooledSlicedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends AbstractUnpooledSlicedByteBuf to provide unpooled-specific typed accessor overrides that forward to the parent buffer.

nodes.name: UnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Direct ByteBuf that uses sun.misc.Unsafe address arithmetic for faster access than the standard NIO-based UnpooledDirectByteBuf.

nodes.name: UnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends UnpooledDirectByteBuf by caching the native memory address and delegating typed reads/writes to UnsafeByteBufUtil for performance.

nodes.name: UnpooledUnsafeHeapByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Heap ByteBuf variant that uses sun.misc.Unsafe to perform array access without bounds checks, trading safety for throughput.

nodes.name: UnpooledUnsafeHeapByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends UnpooledHeapByteBuf and routes typed accessors through UnsafeByteBufUtil's array offset arithmetic for faster reads/writes.

nodes.name: UnpooledUnsafeNoCleanerDirectByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Unsafe direct ByteBuf variant that allocates without a JDK Cleaner, requiring explicit deallocation via PlatformDependent.freeDirectNoCleaner.

nodes.name: UnpooledUnsafeNoCleanerDirectByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends UnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf and overrides allocate/free/reallocate to skip the JDK Cleaner mechanism entirely.

nodes.name: UnreleasableByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Wrapper that makes a ByteBuf immune to release() calls, useful for sharing immutable singleton buffers (e.g., constants) safely across consumers.

nodes.name: UnreleasableByteBuf
nodes.summary: Decorating ByteBuf whose retain/release/touch methods are no-ops, preventing the underlying buffer from being deallocated.

nodes.name: UnsafeByteBufUtil.java
nodes.summary: Static utility providing sun.misc.Unsafe-backed primitive read/write, copy, and search routines used by unsafe ByteBuf implementations.

nodes.name: UnsafeByteBufUtil
nodes.summary: Package-private helper offering static getByte/setLong/setBytes/find primitives that operate on raw native addresses or array offsets via Unsafe.

nodes.name: UnsafeDirectSwappedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Swapped-order wrapper specialised for unsafe direct buffers, using UnsafeByteBufUtil to read big-endian-stored values as little-endian (or vice versa).

nodes.name: UnsafeDirectSwappedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends AbstractUnsafeSwappedByteBuf and reads/writes primitives directly at the unsafe native address with swapped endianness.

nodes.name: UnsafeHeapSwappedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Swapped-order wrapper specialised for unsafe heap buffers, performing primitive reads/writes via Unsafe array offsets with endianness flipped.

nodes.name: UnsafeHeapSwappedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends AbstractUnsafeSwappedByteBuf and uses the buffer's heap array plus base offset for swapped-endian primitive access.

nodes.name: VarHandleByteBufferAccess.java
nodes.summary: JDK 9+ VarHandle-based accessor for reading and writing multi-byte primitives on java.nio.ByteBuffer with explicit endianness control.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses java.lang.invoke.VarHandle to avoid Unsafe while still achieving direct memory access semantics.

nodes.name: VarHandleByteBufferAccess
nodes.summary: Holds VarHandle instances for short/int/long/float/double in both byte orders and exposes static get/set helpers over java.nio.ByteBuffer.

nodes.name: WrappedByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Base decorator that forwards every ByteBuf method to a wrapped delegate; intended for subclasses that intercept specific operations.

nodes.name: WrappedByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends ByteBuf and delegates the entire 180+ method surface to an inner buf field, providing a foundation for selective override decorators.

nodes.name: WrappedCompositeByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Decorator that wraps a CompositeByteBuf and forwards all composite-specific and standard ByteBuf operations; serves as a base for composite decorators.

nodes.name: WrappedCompositeByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends CompositeByteBuf and forwards every composite plus ByteBuf method to a wrapped instance, enabling layered composite decorators.

nodes.name: WrappedUnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf.java
nodes.summary: Tiny package-private adapter that wraps an existing native memory address as an unsafe direct ByteBuf without owning the memory.

nodes.name: WrappedUnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf
nodes.summary: Extends UnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf to expose a constructor accepting a raw memory address and length for foreign-allocated regions.

nodes.name: module-info.yml
nodes.summary: JPMS module descriptor metadata for netty-buffer expressed in YAML, used by Netty's build to generate the actual module-info.java.

nodes.name: native-image.properties
nodes.summary: GraalVM native-image build arguments that configure initialization classes and runtime hints required when compiling Netty applications to native binaries.

nodes.name: reflect-config.json
nodes.summary: GraalVM native-image reflection metadata declaring which Netty buffer classes need reflective access at runtime in native binaries.

layers.name: Public API & ByteBuf Hierarchy
layers.description: Public ByteBuf interface, holder/processor contracts, abstract base classes, and derived/swapped/wrapped variants that form the public surface of the netty-buffer module.

layers.name: Allocators
layers.description: ByteBufAllocator contracts and concrete allocator implementations (Pooled, Unpooled, Adaptive) that hand out ByteBuf instances and expose allocator metrics.

layers.name: Pooled Buffer Implementation
layers.description: Internal pooled allocator machinery: PoolArena, PoolChunk, PoolSubpage, PoolThreadCache, SizeClasses, and pooled ByteBuf subclasses backing PooledByteBufAllocator.

layers.name: Unpooled & Composite Buffers
layers.description: Unpooled heap/direct ByteBuf implementations, composite/empty/read-only/unreleasable variants, and the Unpooled factory entry point.

layers.name: Leak Detection
layers.description: Leak-aware ByteBuf wrappers (Simple/Advanced) used by the resource-leak detector to track buffer ownership at runtime.

layers.name: Unsafe & Low-Level Access
layers.description: sun.misc.Unsafe-based byte access helpers, VarHandle ByteBuffer access, and ByteBufUtil low-level primitives used across pooled and unpooled implementations.

layers.name: Allocator Events & Metrics
layers.description: Allocator/chunk/buffer lifecycle event types and metric interfaces (allocate, free, reallocate, chunk info) exposed for instrumentation.

layers.name: Search Algorithms
layers.description: KMP, Bitap, and Aho-Corasick search-processor factories in io.netty.buffer.search used by ByteBufUtil.indexOf* operations.

layers.name: Build & Native-Image Config
layers.description: Maven pom.xml, module-info, and GraalVM native-image configuration resources for the netty-buffer module.

tour.title: Module Manifest & Layout
tour.description: Start with the Maven descriptor and JPMS module metadata that define netty-buffer as a standalone artifact. The pom.xml declares the module's only runtime dependencies (netty-common for PlatformDependent / leak-detection plumbing and JCTools for lock-free queues used by pooled caches), and module-info.yml feeds Netty's build the data it needs to generate module-info.java. Together they frame the rest of the tour: this is a self-contained library that exposes the io.netty.buffer package and nothing else.

tour.title: The ByteBuf Abstraction
tour.description: ByteBuf is the central type the whole module exists to provide. Unlike java.nio.ByteBuffer it carries separate reader and writer indices, supports reference counting for deterministic release, and exposes slicing/duplication for zero-copy reuse. ByteBufConvertible and ByteBufHolder define how higher layers (channels, codecs) interact with the buffer without coupling to a specific implementation.
tour.languageLesson: Java abstract classes vs interfaces: ByteBuf is an abstract class (not an interface) because Netty needs the public API to evolve with default behavior and protected helpers without breaking every implementation. The ByteBufHolder interface, in contrast, is a pure contract used by codecs to wrap a payload.

tour.title: Skeleton Implementations & Reference Counting
tour.description: Concrete buffers do not implement ByteBuf directly; they extend a chain of abstract skeletons. AbstractByteBuf centralizes index management, bounds checking, and the read/write dispatch that turns the public API into a few protected primitives subclasses must implement. AbstractReferenceCountedByteBuf layers atomic, lock-free retain/release on top using ReferenceCountUpdater so buffers can be safely shared across threads.

tour.title: Allocator Contract & Selection Logic
tour.description: ByteBufAllocator is the factory every caller in Netty depends on. AbstractByteBufAllocator implements the policy that is identical across pooled and unpooled allocators: choose heap vs direct based on the platform, install the leak-detection wrapper when enabled, and grow buffers via calculateNewCapacity. Subclasses only have to implement newHeapBuffer / newDirectBuffer.

tour.title: Unpooled Path & the Unpooled Factory
tour.description: The simplest allocator just news up a fresh buffer on every call. UnpooledByteBufAllocator picks between heap (byte[]) and direct (NIO ByteBuffer) variants, with optional unsafe specialisations when sun.misc.Unsafe is available. The Unpooled static facade is the entry point most user code writes against; it is the easiest place to see how a buffer goes from a factory call to a concrete object on the heap.

tour.title: Pooled Allocator: jemalloc-Style Memory Management
tour.description: PooledByteBufAllocator is Netty's high-throughput allocator, modelled on Facebook's jemalloc. It reserves large chunks from the OS once, then carves them into runs (PoolChunk's buddy allocator) and subpages (PoolSubpage's bitmap), bucketed into size classes precomputed by SizeClasses. PoolThreadCache keeps recently freed regions on the calling thread to bypass the contended arena lock on the hot path.
tour.languageLesson: Object pooling in Java only pays off when allocation cost dominates and GC pressure is real. Netty uses an arena + per-thread cache pattern: contention happens only on cache miss, and freed buffers go back to the thread-local cache so the next allocation is a pointer bump rather than a full pool traversal.

tour.title: Pooled ByteBuf Recycling
tour.description: PooledByteBuf is the abstract base for buffers handed out by PoolArena: each one remembers its owning chunk and an opaque handle so it can return its memory to the right pool on release. PooledHeapByteBuf and PooledDirectByteBuf are the safe variants; PooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf is the production hot path that uses raw memory addresses. Releasing one of these does not free memory; it just hands the region back to PoolArena or PoolThreadCache.

tour.title: Composite, Derived & Wrapped Buffers
tour.description: Several variants exist to support zero-copy patterns: CompositeByteBuf stitches many child buffers into a single virtual view, AbstractDerivedByteBuf is the base for slice/duplicate views that share storage with a parent, and WrappedByteBuf is a pass-through decorator other wrappers extend. UnreleasableByteBuf makes a buffer immune to release(), which is how Netty exposes constant singletons safely.

tour.title: Leak Detection
tour.description: Reference counting is fragile: a single missed release() leaks memory in production. AbstractByteBufAllocator from Step 4 wraps every allocation in SimpleLeakAwareByteBuf, which registers a ResourceLeakTracker that fires if the buffer is garbage-collected without being released. AdvancedLeakAwareByteBuf goes further and records every method call into the tracker so the leak report includes the full access trace, at significant runtime cost.

tour.title: Unsafe Low-Level Access
tour.description: The fastest read/write path bypasses bounds checks and uses sun.misc.Unsafe to touch memory directly. UnsafeByteBufUtil is the shared static toolkit that the unsafe-variant buffers (UnpooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf, PooledUnsafeDirectByteBuf, the swapped wrappers) all delegate to. VarHandleByteBufferAccess is the modern JDK 9+ alternative used when Unsafe is unavailable.
tour.languageLesson: sun.misc.Unsafe lets Java code do C-style memory access (allocateMemory, getLong(address), putByte(address,b)) at the cost of giving up bounds checking and JVM safety guarantees. Netty isolates Unsafe usage behind PlatformDependent and these *Unsafe* classes so the rest of the codebase still compiles and runs on JVMs where Unsafe is locked down.

tour.title: ByteBufUtil & Search Algorithms
tour.description: ByteBufUtil is the static hub for cross-cutting buffer operations: hex dump, equals/compare, UTF-8 encode/decode, hashing, and indexOf. The io.netty.buffer.search subpackage provides the heavier-duty alternatives ByteBufUtil delegates to: KMP and Bitap for single-needle search, and Aho-Corasick for matching many needles in a single pass.

tour.title: GraalVM Native-Image Support
tour.description: Closing the tour with the deployment story: when a Netty application is compiled to a GraalVM native binary, the build needs hints about which classes use reflection and which must be initialized at build versus runtime. native-image.properties pins the initialization strategy and reflect-config.json lists the buffer classes that have to remain reflectively accessible. Without these files an AOT-compiled Netty app would fail at startup with missing-class or initialization errors.
tour.languageLesson: GraalVM native-image is closed-world: every class loaded at runtime must be reachable from the static call graph. Anything done via Class.forName or Constructor.newInstance has to be declared up front in reflect-config.json, and classes with side-effecting <clinit> blocks need an explicit --initialize-at-run-time entry to avoid baking host state into the binary.
